4
How the Client Experience Differs
Assume a family is interested in building an art collection that appreciates and prefers contemporary art
Traditional Art Advisor
Object Recommendation
Damien Hirst
Spots
Rationale
  • Advisor has access to this painting through another dealer or intermediary
  • They like Damien Hirst and believe in his cultural significance
Masterworks Institutional Art Advisory
Object Recommendation
Yayoi Kusama
Nets
14% Historical Appreciation
18% “Discount” to Pre-Acquisition Fair Market Value Appraisal
Rationale
  • Masterworks data models filters for styles of works
  • Kusama was as a high-appreciating artist
  • We have sourced 1,200 Kusama infinity net paintings and negotiated on over 20% of them
  • We pull works with the highest potential discount

5
Our Process
We have a structured, five-phase engagement with your clients
1
Discovery &
Goal Setting
Understand their collection objectives, risk appetite, and aesthetic preferences to define target allocations across periods, artists, and themes
2
Research &
Sourcing
Your clients get access to ML models to identify acquisition targets. Source works through our network of 2,000+ intermediaries and auction houses.
3
Diligence &
Acquisition
We fully diligence every work: provenance, condition, authenticity, comparable sales and exit liquidity. Then we negotiate and execute on the acquisition.
4
Monitoring &
Optimization
We provide ongoing portfolio tracking, along with rebalancing recommendations, exit timing optimization and regular reporting
5
Painting
Sales
When buyer interest exists for works in your collection, Masterworks surfaces those opportunities to you directly

6
Characteristics Overview
Masterworks can generate a custom collection overview tailored to the artist and object characteristics you are looking to acquire

Methodology

Masterworks tracks the current and historical status of artists across a variety of categories. These include commercial maturity and success, artistic style and cultural significance.

Based on these labels we can provide an overview of your current collection.

These categories are highly customizable and can be tailored to your interests.

Commercial Maturity
Emerging ArtistsEstablished ArtistsMature
Artistic Grouping
WesternNon-WesternContemporaryPost-War
Commercial Success
Market is IncreasingMarket is DecliningCollection Concentration
Cultural Significance
Social Media PopularityInstitutional SupportExhibition ScoreMega Gallery Representation

Masterworks — The Leader in Art Investing
We are the world's leading asset manager for art investment products, ranging from private to public vehicles across single assets and funds
Our Expertise

Our mission: making museum-quality art and real asset investing accessible to all investors

Our differentiator: pioneer in art market securitization under SEC qualification

Our team: 100+ years of combined experience across art, finance, and technology

The Approach
Data-Driven

Proprietary analytics and database of 3M+ transactions

Global Access

Direct sourcing via galleries, auction houses, and dealers

Discipline

Selective acquisition with ~2% pass rate on diligence

Platform

End-to-end securitization, storage, and management

9 Years
Since 2017 Firm Inception
$1.2B+
Capital Raised
525+
Investment Vehicles
Executive Leadership Team

Why Art?

Art is the Largest Non-Securitized Asset Class
The total estimated value of art held by individuals is nearly one-third of the size of the private equity market
Private Debt(1)
$1.7 Trillion
2k Firms
Art(2)
$2.6 Trillion
 
Private Real Assets(3)

Sources: Preqin; McKinsey Global Private Markets Review 2024; Deloitte & ArtTactic Art & Finance Report 2025; UBS & Art Basel Global Art Market Report 2024.   (1) Global private-market AUM investing in debt. (2) Global estimated value of art and collectibles held by individuals, based on surveys of wealth allocation to art/collectibles. (3) Global private-market AUM in real estate, natural resources, and infrastructure. (4) Global private-market AUM investing in private equity.

What Drives Art Market Performance?
Secular demand and structural scarcity underpin price growth
Price Growth
Non Correlation
Top 1% Growth

Secular growth in purchasing power among the global UHNW community driving participation

28%
5-Yr UHNWI Growth
Supply Scarcity

Fixed supply contracts as works enter permanent museum collections and institutions

12%
Share of Art Sold Privately to Museums
Global Portability

Physical, mobile store of value that functions across locales and remains currency-agnostic

57%
International Volume Share
Non-Economic Factors

Collector passion and cultural significance operate as sources of uncorrelated returns

198M
Annual Museum Visits
Source: Elliman Insider — The Wealth Report; The Art Basel & UBS Art Market Report 2025; The Art Newspaper. Please see “Important Disclosure Information” section of this presentation.
